NVDA.title: Better Buy: AMD vs. Intel After Intel's Monster Run
NVDA.summary: The article compares Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) and Intel (INTC) after both companies experienced significant stock rallies. While Intel had a "monster run" with a 500% surge, AMD also saw over 300% growth. The analysis suggests AMD is the better buy due to its faster growth, stronger profits, and more favorable valuation despite both companies pursuing opportunities in the AI chip market.

NVDA.title: Qualcomm Rose 6% Today as AI Data Center Hopes Build. Here’s Where the Stock Could Head in 2026
NVDA.summary: Qualcomm (QCOM) stock surged 6% due to growing optimism regarding its AI data center strategy ahead of its Investor Day. JPMorgan raised its price target to $265, anticipating Qualcomm to reveal significant data center revenue targets for 2027 and beyond. However, a valuation model suggests the stock could be overvalued at $226, implying a 20% downside under base-case assumptions, with future performance heavily relying on the growth of non-handset businesses like AI data centers, Automotive, and IoT.

NVDA.title: ON Semiconductor Positioned to Benefit from AI Spending Surge
NVDA.summary: ON Semiconductor is poised for significant growth due to the projected surge in AI inference spending, which is expected to exceed data center infrastructure spending. The company's data center revenue is anticipated to reach $250 million by 2025, growing to $500 million by 2026, driven by demand for power semiconductors in AI, electric vehicles, and industrial markets. Analysts from Citi and Mizuho have raised price targets for ON Semiconductor, citing strong market potential and the impact of agentic AI.

NVDA.title: ON Semiconductor Corp Stock (ON) Closed Up by 7.70% on Jun 20: Key Drivers Unveiled
NVDA.summary: ON Semiconductor Corp (ON) stock rose by 7.70% on June 20, driven by a broad rally in the semiconductor sector, easing macroeconomic pressures, and strong investor sentiment. Financial institution upgrades and new leveraged ETFs contributed to increased visibility and liquidity. The company's strategic investments in silicon carbide and power solutions are seen as key for long-term growth in AI data centers and automotive infrastructure.

NVDA.title: Microchip Technology Inc Stock (MCHP) Closed Up by 6.01% on Jun 20: Facts Behind the Movement
NVDA.summary: Microchip Technology Inc. (MCHP) stock rose 6.01% on June 20, driven by a broad semiconductor rally and the company's strategic shift towards AI-driven data center solutions. Increased order bookings, declining inventory, and upward analyst revisions for earnings also contributed to the positive movement. Despite this, the article notes potential risks including supply bottlenecks in mature-node semiconductors, input cost inflation, elevated debt, and a significant valuation premium suggesting the stock may be overvalued.

NVDA.title: Teradyne Inc Stock (TER) Moved Up by 7.19% on Jun 20: Facts Behind the Movement
NVDA.summary: Teradyne (TER) stock rose 7.19% due to its upcoming inclusion in the Nasdaq-100 Index, strong demand for its semiconductor test equipment driven by the AI boom, and a new multi-year contract with the United States Air Force. Despite a high valuation and insider selling, analyst upgrades and a raised dividend are contributing to market optimism. The company also benefits from its robotics division and strong institutional sponsorship.

NVDA.title: Coherent shares edge up to close out short week, AI optics gains against China indium worries
NVDA.summary: Coherent shares ended a holiday-shortened week up 1.2% ahead of Juneteenth, buoyed by a letter of intent for up to $50 million from the U.S. CHIPS Act to expand its indium phosphide plant for AI data centers. This positive news comes despite new reports highlighting China's tightening inspections on indium exports, a critical metal for AI optical chips, reintroducing supply chain risk into the spotlight. The company posted solid fiscal third-quarter revenues and optimistic guidance, emphasizing its role in the scaling AI data-center infrastructure.

NVDA.title: Austrian Banks Fueled EWO’s Gains; Margin Pressure Now Threatens The Income
NVDA.summary: The iShares MSCI Austria ETF (EWO) has seen significant price appreciation, up 55% in one year, but its income distribution is under threat. The ETF's payouts, largely driven by Austrian banks like Erste Group and Raiffeisen, have already experienced double-digit year-over-year cuts in the last two cycles due to slowing euro area growth and potential European Central Bank rate cuts. Investors seeking consistent income may find EWO's payout pattern uneven, despite its strong total return performance.

MSFT.title: Infosys Ltd Stock (INFY) Closed Down by 9.74% on Jun 20: What Investors Need To Know
MSFT.summary: Infosys Ltd (INFY) shares dropped by 9.74% on June 20, primarily due to Accenture's lowered revenue guidance which signaled a broad slowdown in global enterprise IT spending. This decline reflects a structural shift in client spending from traditional IT projects to targeted AI investments, impacting offshore IT providers. Macroeconomic volatility and technical breakdowns further fueled the sell-off, pushing Infosys to a multi-year low despite its solid fundamentals.

MSFT.title: Is Axon Enterprise Inc. (AXON) a High-Growth Large-Cap Stock to Invest in Now?
MSFT.summary: Axon Enterprise Inc. (AXON) is identified as a high-growth large-cap stock, with Piper Sandler reiterating an Overweight rating and a $674 price target. The company is seen as an operating system for its customers due to platformization, significant international wins, and increasing trials, with AI driving cloud migrations expected to fuel 30% growth. Despite a lack of near-term catalysts beyond execution and product releases, current levels are considered an ideal entry point.

MSFT.title: Expedia Group, Inc. (EXPE) A High-Growth Large -Cap Stock to Buy As it Capitalizes on Surge in Travelling Demand
MSFT.summary: Expedia Group Inc. (EXPE) is highlighted as a high-growth large-cap stock well-positioned to benefit from the increasing demand for consolidated travel bookings on a single platform. Recent global research shows a strong preference among travelers to plan and manage their entire trips through one trusted platform, a trend Expedia is capitalizing on with its expanded Rapid API ecosystem. This platform offers extensive options for flights, car rentals, and trip protection, enabling Expedia to meet the soaring travel demand effectively.

MSFT.title: 5 High Quality Stocks to Buy for the Long Term
MSFT.summary: This article identifies 5 high-quality stocks for long-term investment, with a focus on Vistra Corp. (NYSE: VST). It highlights recent analyst ratings and price target increases for Vistra, noting its position in the power, clean energy, and liquified natural gas sectors. The report suggests Vistra is well-positioned to benefit from the ongoing restructuring of the energy industry in the U.S.

MSFT.title: How Yieldmax Msft Option Income Strategy Etf (MSFO) Affects Rotational Strategy Timing
MSFT.summary: The article analyzes the Yieldmax Msft Option Income Strategy ETF (MSFO), indicating weak sentiment across all time horizons which supports a short bias. It details an exceptional 33.3:1 risk-reward setup targeting a 9.8% gain versus 0.3% risk. The analysis provides specific institutional trading strategies, including long, momentum breakout, and risk hedging (short) options, along with multi-timeframe signal analysis and AI-generated signals for support and resistance levels.

MSFT.title: CDW Corp. Stock - Analyst sentiment and long-term positioning
MSFT.summary: CDW Corp. (US1258961002) is a closely watched US IT solutions provider, with analysts generally maintaining a "Buy"-equivalent assessment due to its stable, cash-generative business model and diversified customer base. The company benefits from continuous IT modernization in enterprises and public institutions, driving demand for its extensive portfolio of products and services, acting as a key channel partner orchestrating solutions from thousands of vendors to approximately 250,000 customers. CDW aims to deepen its role in solution design and lifecycle services amidst digitalization, hybrid work, and rising security requirements.

MSFT.title: Vanguard's VONG or iShares' IJT: Which Growth ETF Should Long-Term Investors Choose?
MSFT.summary: This article compares two growth ETFs, Vanguard Russell 1000 Growth ETF (VONG) and iShares S&P Small-Cap 600 Growth ETF (IJT), for long-term investors. VONG focuses on large-cap technology companies with a lower expense ratio and stronger long-term returns, while IJT targets diversified small-cap growth stocks, emphasizing profitability before inclusion. The analysis concludes that VONG is generally a stronger foundation due to its performance and cost, with IJT serving as a complement for those seeking small-cap exposure with a quality screen.

MSFT.title: Music Business Prospects Assert Sony Group Corporation (SONY) as one of the Best Falling Stocks to Invest In
MSFT.summary: Goldman Sachs has reiterated a Buy rating on Sony Group Corporation (SONY) with a JPY 4,100 price target, noting an increase in companies seeking partnerships and licenses for Sony Music's AI products. Despite copyright infringement lawsuits against some music-generating AI companies, Sony is well-positioned due to its extensive music catalog and strong market share in Latin America. The investment bank suggests investors will observe how Sony monetizes its ventures in the generative AI era.

GOOGL.title: Infosys Ltd Stock (INFY) Closed Down by 9.74% on Jun 20: What Investors Need To Know
GOOGL.summary: Infosys Ltd (INFY) shares dropped by 9.74% on June 20, primarily due to Accenture's lowered revenue guidance which signaled a broad slowdown in global enterprise IT spending. This decline reflects a structural shift in client spending from traditional IT projects to targeted AI investments, impacting offshore IT providers. Macroeconomic volatility and technical breakdowns further fueled the sell-off, pushing Infosys to a multi-year low despite its solid fundamentals.

GOOGL.title: 5,536 Shares in Salesforce Inc. $CRM Acquired by PeakShares LLC
GOOGL.summary: PeakShares LLC has acquired 5,536 shares of Salesforce Inc. ($CRM), valued at $1.47 million, making it the 14th largest holding in their portfolio. Salesforce has shown strong financial performance with better-than-expected quarterly results, a significant share repurchase program, and a recently announced quarterly dividend. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for the stock, with various institutional investors also increasing their stakes in the company.

GOOGL.title: Rigetti (RGTI): Complete Commercial History 2026
GOOGL.summary: This article details the commercial history of Rigetti Computing (Nasdaq: RGTI), a full-stack superconducting quantum computing company, from its founding in 2013 to 2026. It highlights Rigetti's unique vertical integration, including its own chip fabrication facility (Fab-1) and the sale of its Novera quantum processing unit. The piece also discusses the company's "fidelity-first" roadmap under CEO Subodh Kulkarni, its financial journey through a SPAC merger and market rallies, and the strategic importance of government funding in validating its onshore manufacturing approach.

GOOGL.title: Eightco Holdings (NASDAQ: ORBS) Reports Total Holdings of Approximately $472 Million, Includes OpenAI, Beast Industries, More Than 16,000 ETH and Over 283 Million WLD Tokens
GOOGL.summary: Eightco Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ: ORBS) has announced total holdings valued at approximately $472 million as of June 17, 2026. These significant holdings include indirect investments in OpenAI and Beast Industries, alongside substantial amounts of 16,278 Ethereum (ETH) and 283 million Worldcoin (WLD) tokens. The company positions itself as a key player providing exposure to the mega-trends of artificial intelligence, digital identity, and the creator economy.

GOOGL.title: WhiteFiber (WYFI) Stock Looks 9% Overvalued After $100 Million AI Infrastructure Loan
GOOGL.summary: WhiteFiber (WYFI) recently secured a $100 million AI infrastructure loan, causing its stock to surge. Despite this positive news and strong recent performance, the stock is considered 9% overvalued based on a narrative fair value of $35.44, trading above its analyst price target. While the company has significant growth potential in its Cloud pipeline, managing complex data center projects and rising debt costs pose risks to its optimistic valuation.

GOOGL.title: LinkedIn's 2026 Top 10 U.S. Companies for Career Growth Highlight JPMorgan, Microsoft, and Amazon
GOOGL.summary: JPMorgan Chase has reclaimed the top spot on LinkedIn's 2026 list of best U.S. companies for career growth, thanks in part to its significant investment in AI training for its workforce. Microsoft entered the top five for the first time due to a change in LinkedIn's criteria, and other tech giants like Amazon and Alphabet also feature prominently. The rankings highlight the increasing importance of AI fluency for job seekers, even as many of these top companies have undergone significant workforce reductions.

GOOGL.title: Honeywell (HON) Stock Weighs Aerospace Spin Plans Against Fair Value Around $229
GOOGL.summary: Honeywell International's stock (HON) is currently trading around $229, appearing fairly valued according to Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis at a 7.8% discount to its estimated intrinsic value of $248.30. Its P/E ratio of 36.6x is above the Industrials sector average but close to its proprietary Fair Ratio, suggesting it's broadly in line with this metric. The article also presents bull and bear case narratives, with fair value estimates ranging from $199.69 to $320.19 per share, influenced by assumptions around aerospace spin-off plans and market dynamics.

GOOGL.title: Prediction: Alphabet Stock Will Outpace the Market in 2026
GOOGL.summary: This article predicts that Alphabet (GOOG/GOOGL) stock will outperform the market in 2026, setting a price target of $445.38 with a "buy" recommendation. The bullish outlook is based on robust 82% earnings growth, a forward P/E of 26, the strong performance of Google Cloud with a $460 billion backlog, and accelerating Search business growth. While acknowledging risks like high capital expenditure and regulatory pressure, the author remains confident in Alphabet's long-term potential.
